parameters.name: output
parameters.label: Output
parameters.description: Fractions or Units affects the channel data that is output from the Clock . Fraction gives convenient 0-1 ramps and Units give integers, like 0-23 for the hours of a day. For example, use Fractions an

parameters.name: hourformat
parameters.label: Hour Format
parameters.description: 12 hour or 24 hour - Causes the hour channel to cycle through 12 or 24 hours. Also affects the AM/PM channel.

parameters.name: houradjust
parameters.label: Hour Adjust
parameters.description: After the Clock reads the current time, it adds Hour Adjust to pretend the current time is different than the actual current time.

parameters.name: startref
parameters.label: Start Reference
parameters.description: The date/time that corresponds to year 0, day 1, hour 0, minute 0. It can be relative to Jan 1, 2000 or to the time that the TouchDesigner process started.

parameters.name: msec
parameters.label: Millisecond
parameters.description: If Output is Units, it is the current millisecond, starting at 0 at the start of a second, going up to 999 at the end of a second. If Output is Fraction, it is the current fraction of a millisecond.

parameters.name: sec
parameters.label: Second
parameters.description: If Output is Units, it is the current second, starting at 0 for on-the-hour, going up to 59. If Output is Fraction, it is the current fraction of a second (45 seconds past the minute gives .75).

parameters.name: min
parameters.label: Minute
parameters.description: If Output is Units, it is the current minute, starting at 0 for on-the-hour, going up to 59. If Output is Fraction, it is the current fraction of a minute (45 seconds past the minute gives .75).

parameters.name: hour
parameters.label: Hour
parameters.description: If Output is Units, it is the current hour, starting at 0 for midnight and affected by AM/PM. If Output is Fraction, it is the current fraction of an hour (15 minutes past the hour gives .25), taking

parameters.name: ampm
parameters.label: AM/PM
parameters.description: 0 if before noon, 1 if after noon.

parameters.name: wday
parameters.label: Day of Week
parameters.description: If Output is Units, it is the actual day of the week, starting with 0 for Monday and 6 for Sunday.

parameters.name: day
parameters.label: Day of Month
parameters.description: If Output is Units, it is the actual day of the month, so on March 20, it is 20. If Output is Fraction, it is fraction of a day of the current moment, so at 6:30 PM, it is .77, taking into account the

parameters.name: yday
parameters.label: Day of Year
parameters.description: If Output is Units, it is the Day of year, starting with 0 for January 1. If it is Fraction, it is the same as Day of Month.

parameters.name: week
parameters.label: Week
parameters.description: Week of the year, starting with 0 for the first week and 51 for the last week of the year.

parameters.name: month
parameters.label: Month
parameters.description: Month of the year, starting with 1 for January and 12 for December.

parameters.name: year
parameters.label: Year
parameters.description: If Output is Units, it is the integer year number relative to the Start , starting at 0, so year 2009 is 9 by default. If Output is Fraction, it is the current fraction of a year, taking into account

parameters.name: northsouth
parameters.label: NS
parameters.description: Set if the Latitude value above is in the north or south hemisphere.

parameters.name: eastwest
parameters.label: EW
parameters.description: Set if the Longitude value above is in the east or west hemisphere.

parameters.name: moonphase
parameters.label: Moon Phase
parameters.description: Outputs the moon phase (0 to 1. .5 is a full moon, 0 and 1 are at the time of the new moon).

parameters.name: sunphase
parameters.label: Sun Phase
parameters.description: (0 to 1, where sunrise=0, sunset = 1, and it reverses down to 0 in time for the sunrise.

parameters.name: sunrise
parameters.label: Sunrise
parameters.description: Outputs the sunrise time (0 to 1, midnight=0, twenty-four hours later = 1).

parameters.name: sunset
parameters.label: Sunset
parameters.description: Outputs the sunset time (0 to 1, midnight=0, twent.y-four hours later = 1).

parameters.name: declination
parameters.label: Declination
parameters.description: (-180 to 180, degrees north/south that the sun is off the equator).

parameters.name: timeslice
parameters.label: Time Slice
parameters.description: Turning this on forces the channels to be " ".  A is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.label: Scope
parameters.description: To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a string on the Common page.

parameters.name: srselect
parameters.label: Sample Rate Match
parameters.description: Handle cases where multiple input CHOPs' sample rates are different. When Resampling occurs, the curves are interpolated according to the Interpolation Method Option, or "Linear" if the Interpolate Op

parameters.name: exportmethod
parameters.label: Export Method
parameters.description: This will determine how to connect the channel to the parameter. Refer to the article for more information.

parameters.name: autoexportroot
parameters.label: Export Root
parameters.description: This path points to the root node where all of the paths that exporting by are relative to.

parameters.name: exporttable
parameters.label: Export Table
parameters.description: The used to hold the export information when using the Table Methods (See above).

parameters.name: longitude2
parameters.label: Longitude2
parameters.description: Enter a longitude (hours/min east/west) of your location. Fractional hours are permitted. The parameter longitude1 is hours, longitude2 is minutes.

parameters.name: leapyear
parameters.label: Leapyear
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: dayselapsed
parameters.label: Dayselapsed
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: latitude2
parameters.label: Latitude2
parameters.description: Enter a latitude (hours/min north/south) of your location. (defaults to Toronto, Canada). Fractional hours are permitted. For example:  43.6532 hours and 0 minutes, is identical to 43 hours and 39 min
